behaviors.description: Out-of-stock products are never returned in recommendation results, even if their relevance score is high.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: if any product with availability=out_of_stock appears in the final returned list, the behavior fails. This failure is most likely at module handoff (availability filter -> ranker -> response assembler), not just inside one pure function. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on AI/LLM output quality. Rejected System because behavior text only states catalog filtering in recommendation generation (no real HTTP/subprocess/OS constraint). Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model non-determinism or multi-agent sequence is described.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender end-to-end in-process with fixture catalog where some out-of-stock items have top relevance scores. Assert final returned page contains zero out-of-stock items after filtering+ranking+pagination wiring.

behaviors.description: Blocked categories from user settings are excluded from recommendations.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: any returned item whose category is in user.blocked_categories violates the behavior. Real failures usually come from settings retrieval/mapping plus filtering interaction, so module wiring is the minimum boundary. No real infra is necessary; in-process fixtures can reproduce. Rejected System: behavior text is about settings-based filtering, not external infrastructure behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: deterministic rule-based exclusion.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process user-profile fixture with explicit blocked categories and mixed catalog. Execute recommendation pipeline and assert no result category intersects blocked set.

behaviors.description: Products with recent browsing signal are ranked above products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: given equal other factors, item with positive recent-browsing feature must score/rank above item with zero browsing feature. This is core ranking logic and can fail within one scoring/ranking function without module interaction. No real infra required; no AI/LLM output dependency is stated. Rejected Integration because behavior text targets algorithm weighting itself, not cross-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Call scoring/ranking function with controlled feature vectors (same purchase/similar-user features, differing browsing feature). Assert browsing-positive candidate receives higher score and higher rank.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page and stable ordering across repeated page-2 requests when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: two identical requests for page=2 against unchanged data return identical ordered IDs, and the IDs correspond to the global rank slice for page 2. Failures typically appear when ranking output and paginator/tie-break logic interact. No required real OS/network/subprocess boundary. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real external infra; in-process module wiring can expose unstable ordering bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Build deterministic in-memory dataset with ties, run full in-process recommendation + pagination twice for page 2 with no state changes, and assert identical ordered IDs and correct offset/limit slice from the ranked list.

behaviors.description: Results include at least one product from an unbrowsed category (serendipity).
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: for a user history covering categories A/B only, top-N output must contain >=1 item with category not in {A,B}. This can be enforced by a diversification/rerank rule in a single ranking component. No real infra needed, and no LLM/model-quality requirement is specified beyond deterministic business rule compliance. Rejected Agentic because behavior text describes a product-rule requirement, not dependence on non-deterministic model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Feed reranker with candidate set and user history where all high-score items are from browsed categories plus some unbrowsed candidates. Assert final top-N contains at least one unbrowsed-category item.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users get popularity-based recommendations instead of empty results.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: when user has no purchase/browsing history, result set is non-empty and ranked by global popularity source. Failure usually occurs at handoff between user-history detection and fallback strategy selection/data source. No real infra is required; local fixtures cover it. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/process/OS effects. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: fallback criterion is deterministic.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with new-user profile (no events) plus popularity table fixture. Assert recommender chooses popularity fallback path, returns non-empty list, and ordering matches popularity scores.

behaviors.description: Click tracking stores correct product ID, list position, and timestamp for each click.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: persisted click event fields equal the clicked recommendation context (product_id, rank position, event time). Failure is typically in mapper/controller -> event store interaction, not pure local logic only. Can be caught with in-process repository and injected clock; real OS/network not required. Rejected System: behavior text asks field correctness, not real external transport guarantees.
behaviors.test_description: Execute recommendation request, simulate click on a known position, write tracking event through normal service path into test DB/repo with fixed clock, then assert stored fields exactly match expected product ID, position, and timestamp.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: end-to-end request latency p95 (or defined single-run metric) exceeds 200ms for catalog size 100,000. This boundary depends on real runtime characteristics (process scheduling, memory/cache behavior, actual I/O path if present), which mocks can hide. MOCK-HIDE applies: in-process stubs for heavy components can falsely pass. Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly asserts wall-clock performance under load-size constraint, which requires real execution environment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommender service in a real process with 100k-item dataset representative of production schema, issue timed recommendation requests, and assert measured latency metric stays below 200ms threshold.

behaviors.description: Purchased recommended products are deprioritized in later recommendation requests.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: after purchase event for product P, subsequent recommendations for same user rank P lower than before (or below deprioritization threshold). Failure occurs at state handoff between purchase-history update and ranking features, requiring module integration. No real OS/network/subprocess required if purchase and recommendation modules are wired in-process. Rejected System because behavior text focuses on cross-module state propagation, not external infra semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Generate baseline recommendations, record rank of product P, inject purchase event through normal purchase-history path, rerun recommendations, and assert P rank decreases according to business rule.

behaviors.description: Recommendations are deterministic for unchanged user state across repeated requests.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: two back-to-back requests with identical user state and catalog snapshot return identical ordered product IDs. Determinism failures usually emerge from interaction between ranking, tie-breaking, and state/cache layers rather than a single pure function only. No required real infra; can be validated in-process with fixed seeds/clocks. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess effects. Rejected Agentic because no non-deterministic AI model output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: With frozen user state, catalog snapshot, and deterministic seed/time controls, call recommendation endpoint/service twice through normal in-process module wiring and assert identical ranked ID list.
